message:
fix(frankenpandas): forward fp-io sql-* features to umbrella (br-l6nr / fd90.58)

Self-review surfaced an umbrella feature-forwarding gap: fp-io
exposes sql-sqlite (default), sql-postgresql, sql-mysql features
for opt-in/opt-out of SQL backend deps, but the frankenpandas
umbrella crate had no [features] section at all. Users adding
frankenpandas to their Cargo.toml had no way to opt out of
rusqlite — fp-io was always pulled with default features.

Fix:
- Pin the fp-io dep with default-features = false so the umbrella
  controls the feature set itself.
- Add [features] section forwarding sql-sqlite / sql-postgresql /
  sql-mysql to fp-io's matching feature flags.
- default = ["sql-sqlite"] keeps behavior unchanged for existing
  users; --no-default-features now actually opts out at the
  umbrella level.

Verified: umbrella passes 5 tests under default features and 5
tests under --no-default-features. Explicit opt-in
(--no-default-features --features sql-sqlite) compiles cleanly.
Clippy -D warnings clean both profiles. ubs Critical=0.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
